gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Guarantee tag on the honesty lattice `Exact ⊐ Proven ⊐ Empirical ⊐ Declared` (RFC-0016 §4.1 C2;"] # [doc = " VR-5). A standalone enum whose variants mirror the lattice names; [`GuaranteeTag::as_str`] yields"] # [doc = " the matching `'static` string, so rows are assertable in tests without depending on the core"] # [doc = " lattice type."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum GuaranteeTag { # [doc = " No accuracy/precision/probability semantics — the honest floor `Exact` (unused here)."] Exact , # [doc = " Established by a checked side-condition theorem (VR-5; unused here)."] Proven , # [doc = " Holds over a generated corpus (proptest); not `Proven` (VR-5; unused here)."] Empirical , # [doc = " Asserted without a checked basis; always FLAGGED (VR-5). Every `std-sys` floor op is this."] Declared , }
gaps.reason: enum variant/constructor `Exact` collides with a Mycelium reserved word — emitting it verbatim would fail to parse (the lexer tokenizes it as a keyword, not an identifier); no sanctioned auto-rename in this PoC, so flagged rather than emitted (G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet: impl GuaranteeTag { # [doc = " Human-readable name matching the lattice notation (`\"Declared\"`, etc.)."] # [must_use] pub fn as_str (self) -> & 'static str { match self { GuaranteeTag :: Exact => "Exact" , GuaranteeTag :: Proven => "Proven" , GuaranteeTag :: Empirical => "Empirical" , GuaranteeTag :: Declared => "Declared" , } } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (1 sub-issue(s)): impl method `as_str` signature: unsupported Rust type form `& 'static str`

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Fallibility classification for an exported op (C1)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Fallibility { # [doc = " The op cannot fail for any well-formed input (it has no `Result`/`Option` failure path)."] Total , # [doc = " The op returns an explicit `Result`/`Option`; the error set is named in `error_set`."] Fallible , }
gaps.reason: dropped non-doc attribute(s) on enum `Fallibility`: # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)]

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One row in the `std-sys` syscall-floor guarantee matrix (RFC-0016 §4.5)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct MatrixRow { # [doc = " Module-qualified exported operation name (e.g. `\"io::write_out\"`)."] pub op : & 'static str , # [doc = " Guarantee tag (VR-5) — `Declared` for every floor op."] pub guarantee : GuaranteeTag , # [doc = " Fallibility: total or fallible."] pub fallibility : Fallibility , # [doc = " The explicit error set (empty string for total ops)."] pub error_set : & 'static str , # [doc = " Declared effects (C6)."] pub effects : & 'static str , }
gaps.reason: struct `MatrixRow` uses named fields — no record/product-type surface (only a single-ctor positional shape maps to `type_item`)
top-level `const MATRIX` — no const item production in the grammar (`item` covers use/default/type/trait/impl/fn/object/lower/derive only)

gaps.snippet:
# [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: { Fallibility , GuaranteeTag , MATRIX } ; # [doc = " Every exported syscall-floor op appears in the matrix exactly once. Guard: adding or removing"] # [doc = " a floor op without updating the matrix makes this fail."] # [test] fn matrix_covers_every_floor_op_once () { let expected = ["io::read_to_end" , "io::read_to_end_capped" , "io::read_line" , "io::write_out" , "io::write_err" , "io::flush_out" , "fs::read" , "fs::write" , "fs::exists" , "fs::create_dir_all" , "fs::remove_file" , "sys::exit" , "sys::get_env" , "sys::args" , "rand::fill_bytes" , "time::wall_nanos" , "time::mono_nanos" , "time::sleep_nanos" , "math::sin" , "math::cos" , "math::tan" , "math::asin" , "math::acos" , "math::atan" , "math::atan2" , "math::exp" , "math::exp2" , "math::ln" , "math::log2" , "math::log10" , "math::sqrt" , "math::cbrt" ,] ; for name in & expected { let n = MATRIX . iter () . filter (| r | r . op == * name) . count () ; assert_eq ! (n , 1 , "op {name:?} must appear exactly once in MATRIX (found {n})") ; } assert_eq ! (MATRIX . len () , expected . len () , "MATRIX has unexpected extra/missing rows") ; } # [doc = " Every floor op is `Declared` (VR-5). Guard: upgrading any row to `Empirical`/`Proven`/`Exact`"] # [doc = " without a checked basis makes this fail — the honest floor for an unaudited host wrapper."] # [test] fn every_row_is_declared () { for row in MATRIX { assert_eq ! (row . guarantee , GuaranteeTag :: Declared , "op {:?} is not Declared — std-sys is the unaudited OS floor; an upgrade needs its \
                 own checked Empirical/Proven basis (VR-5)" , row . op) ; } } # [doc = " Fallible ops name a non-empty error set; total ops have an empty one (C1 / never-silent G2)."] # [test] fn fallibility_and_error_set_are_consistent () { for row in MATRIX { match row . fallibility { Fallibility :: Total => assert ! (row . error_set . is_empty () , "total op {:?} must have an empty error_set (C1)" , row . op) , Fallibility :: Fallible => assert ! (! row . error_set . is_empty () , "fallible op {:?} must name its error set (C1 / G2)" , row . op) , } } } # [doc = " Every row declares a non-empty effect (C6) — the floor is the OS-contact surface, so no op is"] # [doc = " silently effect-free except the pure libm `math::*` ops (effect `none`)."] # [test] fn effects_are_declared () { for row in MATRIX { assert ! (! row . effects . is_empty () , "op {:?} must declare an effect (C6)" , row . op) ; } let wall = MATRIX . iter () . find (| r | r . op == "time::wall_nanos") . expect ("wall row") ; assert ! (wall . effects . contains ("entropy") , "time::wall_nanos must declare the entropy effect (RT3 — civil time is an entropy source)") ; let fill = MATRIX . iter () . find (| r | r . op == "rand::fill_bytes") . expect ("fill row") ; assert_eq ! (fill . effects , "entropy" , "rand::fill_bytes must declare effect `entropy`") ; } }
gaps.reason: #[cfg(test)] item — out of scope for this PoC's transpilation surface (excluded from the expressible-fraction denominator, but recorded, never silently skipped)
